title: Matched-generator QA compression on exact lawful discrete traces — CONSISTENT with QA_CORE_SPEC.v1 generator-relative organization
_fixture_purpose: PASS fixture: repo-native empirical observation combining the real qa_step trace experiment and the real family [40] reachability compression experiments, including the tiny-trace microtrace fix. The narrow claim is that for exact lawful discrete traces generated by a declared QA family, a matched QA codec compresses best by encoding the governing law plus seed/control data. This is not a universal theorem for noisy, mixed-family, or fractional observer-projection cases.

observation.summary: Across two real exact discrete QA families, matched-generator law compression beat strong generic baselines. For four real qa_step chains of 193 states each, the qa_step-native codec compressed every chain to 11 bytes, beating brotli baselines of 17-38 bytes. For real family [40] reachability traces over Caps(30,30), the aggregate lawful corpus of 231300 states compressed to 546 bytes with qa_codec+zstd versus 2920 bytes for xz_9e, while a minimal qa_microtrace codec fixed the tiny-trace regime and beat brotli on all five representative 257-state lawful runs with 8-10 byte encodings versus 12-16 bytes. Interpretation: generator-relative, scale-aware QA law encoding is strongly supported for exact lawful discrete traces.

parent_cert.claim: lawful generator structure carries genuine information about trajectory organization, so QA state evolution is organized by the chosen generator algebra rather than arbitrary relabelling

evidence.description: Real qa_step chains compress to their law description under a matched generator codec
evidence.value: Four 193-state real qa_step chains: qa_step_codec=11 B; best generic baseline brotli_11=17 B, 33 B, 38 B, 38 B

evidence.description: Real family [40] aggregate lawful corpus shows a strong matched-generator QA advantage
evidence.value: 231300-state aggregate reachability corpus: qa_codec+zstd=546 B vs best baseline xz_9e=2920 B (-81.3%)

evidence.description: Scale-aware microtrace encoding fixes the tiny lawful trace regime
evidence.value: Five 257-state representative reachability runs: qa_microtrace_codec=8-10 B vs best brotli_11 baselines=12-16 B

evidence.description: The repeated pattern across two real families supports a generator-relative compression principle for exact lawful discrete traces
evidence.value: when the trace family is matched and the packaging is scale-aware, law encoding outperforms raw-stream byte compression

evidence.description: The observation remains intentionally narrow and does not certify a universal theorem
evidence.value: mixed/noisy traces and exact-rational observer-projection compression remain open investigation tracks

open_questions: How should mixed-family and partially lawful traces be decomposed into lawful components plus residuals under a matched codec matrix?
Does the same generator-relative compression principle hold across additional QA families beyond qa_step and family [40]?
Can continuous or sampled signals be decomposed exactly into a fractional QA lawful component plus observer-projection residual without using floats?

validation_checks.description: observation.source is a known source type
verdict is one of {CONSISTENT, CONTRADICTS, PARTIAL, INCONCLUSIVE}
verdict==CONTRADICTS implies nonempty fail_ledger (not applicable here)
